description: Fix documentation inaccuracies found during systematic audit of all docs pages against the source code. Covers a phantom config field, missing browser tool prerequisites and parameter details, and an inaccurate TypeScript explanation.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Remove phantom port config field and fix port resolution order
userStories.description: As a user reading the configuration reference, I want the documented config schema to match the actual code so that I don't add a non-existent port field to my config.json.
acceptanceCriteria: The `port` row is removed from the Config Fields table in configuration.mdx (the row with type `number`, default `9515`)
The port resolution order text no longer references `config.json port` — the correct order is: `--port` flag → `OPENTABS_PORT` env var → default `9515`
The example config JSON block does NOT contain a `port` field (verify it was already absent)
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx

The OpentabsConfig interface in platform/mcp-server/src/config.ts (lines 40-51) has NO `port` field. The actual fields are: localPlugins, tools, browserToolPolicy, permissions, skipConfirmation.

Line 28-31 of configuration.mdx has a table row documenting a `port` field with type `number` and default `9515`. Remove this entire row.

Line 31 contains the port resolution order text referencing `config.json port`. Update to remove that step — the actual resolution is: --port flag → OPENTABS_PORT env var → default 9515. This resolution logic lives in platform/cli/src/parse-port.ts.

The example config block (around lines 68-93) already does not contain a port field, so no change needed there.

userStories.title: Add missing browser tool prerequisites and parameter details
userStories.description: As a user of the browser tools, I want accurate documentation of prerequisites and parameter constraints so that I can use tools correctly without trial and error.
acceptanceCriteria: The `browser_get_console_logs` description mentions that `browser_enable_network_capture` must be active on the tab first (the debugger captures both network requests and console output)
The `browser_scroll` description mentions the `container` parameter for scrolling within a specific scrollable element
The `browser_get_page_html` description mentions the default max length of 200,000 characters
The `browser_get_resource_content` description mentions the default max length of 500,000 characters
The `plugin_analyze_site` description mentions the `waitSeconds` maximum constraint of 25 seconds
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/reference/browser-tools.mdx

1. browser_get_console_logs (around line 212): The source at platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/get-console-logs.ts explicitly states in its description that browser_enable_network_capture must be active. Add a note like: 'Requires `browser_enable_network_capture` to be active on the tab (the debugger captures both network and console output).'

2. browser_scroll (around line 126): The source at platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/scroll.ts has a `container` parameter (CSS selector for a scrollable container element). Add mention of this parameter.

3. browser_get_page_html (around line 60): Source at platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/get-page-html.ts shows maxLength defaults to 200,000. Add '(default 200,000 chars)' or similar.

4. browser_get_resource_content (around line 238): Source at platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/get-resource-content.ts shows maxLength defaults to 500,000. Add '(default 500,000 chars)' or similar.

5. plugin_analyze_site (around line 294): Source at platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/analyze-site.ts shows waitSeconds has .max(25). Update to mention 'default 5, max 25'.

userStories.title: Fix inaccurate override keyword explanation in resources-prompts guide
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the explanation of why the override keyword is needed to be technically accurate so that I understand TypeScript's noImplicitOverride rule correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: The override explanation for `resources` (around line 158) no longer says 'optional property with a default value on the base class' — instead it correctly explains that `noImplicitOverride` requires the keyword when overriding any inherited property, even ones without default values
The override explanation for `prompts` (around line 376) is updated with the same correction
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx

Line 158 currently says:
`override` is required because `resources` is an optional property with a default value on the base class — TypeScript's `noImplicitOverride` rule enforces it. Omitting `override` causes a TS4114 type error at build time.

This is inaccurate. The base class (platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts line 205) declares `readonly resources?: ResourceDefinition[]` with NO default value — it's just an optional property. The `override` keyword is required because the project's tsconfig uses `noImplicitOverride: true`, which requires the keyword when overriding any inherited class member, even optional properties without default values.

Replace with something like:
`override` is required because TypeScript's `noImplicitOverride` rule (enabled in the project's tsconfig) requires the keyword when overriding any inherited class member. Omitting `override` causes a TS4114 type error at build time.

The same inaccurate text appears at line 376 for prompts — apply the same fix there.
